methodology: 10 topics x 3 tiers = 30 evals, 27 with assertions (77 total)

evals.name: swift-testing-simple
evals.prompt: What's the difference between `#expect()` and `try #require()` in Swift Testing?

assertions.text: Warns against pre-evaluating expressions to Bool variables before passing to #expect (loses diagnostic context)
Warns against overusing #require for non-precondition checks (hides multiple failures)

evals.name: swift-testing-medium
evals.prompt: I want to test the same login function with 10 different username/password combinations. What's the cleanest way in Swift Testing?

assertions.text: Shows parameterized test syntax with tuple or custom type
Mentions .tags for filtering test subsets
Warns about argument count limits or shows zip() for multiple collections
Warns against using for-loops inside a single @Test (use parameterized tests instead)

evals.name: swift-testing-complex

assertions.text: Identifies XCTAssert in @Test silently passes (no XCTestCase context)
Explains confirmation() counts when CLOSURE RETURNS, not when callback fires
Shows fix: use #expect for assertions, withCheckedContinuation for callbacks
Identifies framework mixing danger (XCTAssert in @Test context)
Warns about inverse direction (#expect in XCTestCase also silently ignored)
Provides decision tree or guidance for when to use confirmation() vs withCheckedContinuation

evals.name: xctest-migration-simple
evals.prompt: How do I convert `XCTAssertEqual(a, b)` to Swift Testing?

assertions.text: Mentions Swift Testing provides better failure messages with expression context
Provides a full assertion mapping table (XCTAssertNil, XCTAssertThrowsError, XCTFail, etc.)
Mentions XCTAssertEqual accuracy gap (no direct Swift Testing equivalent for floating-point tolerance)
Warns about pre-evaluating Bool pitfall when migrating assertions

evals.name: xctest-migration-medium
evals.prompt: I have an XCTest file with setUp(), tearDown(), and XCTestExpectation waits. How do I migrate it to Swift Testing?

assertions.text: tearDown -> deinit (but warns tearDown is not exactly equivalent)
Provides complete lifecycle mapping table (setUp, tearDown, XCTestExpectation, isInverted, expectedFulfillmentCount)
Maps inverted expectations to confirmation(expectedCount: 0)

evals.name: xctest-migration-complex

assertions.text: Explains XCTest parallel ran in SEPARATE PROCESSES, Swift Testing runs in ONE PROCESS
Fix for races: eliminate static state or @Suite(.serialized)
Classifies the double-execution issue with explicit severity level

evals.name: async-testing-simple

assertions.text: Classifies this issue with explicit severity level

evals.name: async-testing-medium
evals.prompt: My test for `isLoading == true` is flaky - sometimes passes, sometimes fails. The ViewModel calls `await repository.fetchItems()`. How do I reliably test the loading state?

assertions.text: Explains Task.yield() inside mock for deterministic suspension

evals.name: async-testing-complex

assertions.text: Option: withMainSerialExecutor to serialize Task execution
Explains mock must use Task.yield() to create deterministic suspension point for withMainSerialExecutor

evals.name: observable-testing-simple
evals.prompt: How do I test an @Observable ViewModel's state changes in Swift Testing?

assertions.text: For async changes: withObservationTracking or confirmation
Explains withObservationTracking has willSet semantics (sees value BEFORE change)

evals.name: observable-testing-medium

assertions.text: Shows @Suite @MainActor struct CartViewModelTests syntax
Alternative: await MainActor.run {} as workaround

evals.name: observable-testing-complex

assertions.text: Uses withObservationTracking in a loop or multiple times
Explains willSet semantics - tracking sees value BEFORE change
Alternative: confirmation(expectedCount: 3) with observation
Addresses timing: mock should yield to allow state assertions

evals.name: tca-testing-simple
evals.prompt: What's a TestStore in TCA and why should I use it?

assertions.text: Mentions TCA >= 1.12 requirement for Swift Testing compatibility (silent pass bug in earlier versions)
Explains @MainActor is required on test functions using TestStore
Mentions unimplemented dependency error (TCA fails tests when a dependency has no test implementation)

evals.name: tca-testing-medium

assertions.text: Shows await store.receive(.loginResponse) syntax
Uses case key path: .receive(\.loginResponse)

evals.name: tca-testing-complex

assertions.text: Override dependency: withDependencies { $0.continuousClock = testClock }
TCA + Swift Testing silent pass: requires TCA >= 1.12
Shows testClock.advance(by: .seconds(3)) to trigger 3 ticks
Provides verification test (intentionally wrong assertion) to confirm failures are reported in Swift Testing
Distinguishes ImmediateClock vs TestClock use cases (timing matters vs just completing async)

evals.name: ui-testing-simple
evals.prompt: How do I wait for a button to appear in XCUITest?

assertions.text: Warns about checking return value of waitForExistence (discarding it leads to confusing crashes)
Distinguishes exists vs isHittable (exists true for off-screen elements)
Shows reusable waitAndTap() extension or convenience pattern

evals.name: ui-testing-medium
evals.prompt: My XCUITest is flaky because a system alert for location permission blocks the flow. How do I handle system alerts?

assertions.text: Must tap on app element after adding monitor to trigger it
Alternative: reset authorization in setUp via launchArguments
Warns about smart/curly quote gotcha in system alert button titles (e.g. Don\u2019t Allow)
Mentions monitor registration order (reverse order - last registered fires first)

evals.name: ui-testing-complex
evals.prompt: Our XCUITest suite has 200 tests and takes 45 minutes. Tests are brittle - small UI changes break many tests. How do we improve maintainability and speed?

assertions.text: Run in parallel with Test Plans
Launch arguments to skip animations, pre-seed state

evals.name: snapshot-testing-simple
evals.prompt: How do I set up snapshot testing for SwiftUI views?

assertions.text: Mentions UIHostingController requirement for wrapping SwiftUI views (assertSnapshot works with UIViewController)
Shows withSnapshotTesting(record:) API for controlling recording behavior (.missing, .all, .never)
Lists what NOT to snapshot (dynamic content, timestamps, animations)

evals.name: snapshot-testing-medium
evals.prompt: My snapshot tests pass locally but fail on CI with tiny pixel differences. How do I fix this?

assertions.text: Pin to specific device/size: .image(layout: .device(config: .iPhone13))
Set precision parameter: .image(precision: 0.99)

evals.name: snapshot-testing-complex

assertions.text: Identifies that assertSnapshot uses XCTAssert under the hood - same framework mixing issue
Shows .image(traits: .init(userInterfaceStyle: .dark))
Identifies the silent pass as same root cause as framework mixing (library wrapping XCTFail)

evals.name: integration-testing-medium
evals.prompt: My Core Data tests are slow and sometimes fail with 'store already in use'. How do I isolate Core Data in tests?

assertions.text: Use in-memory store: NSInMemoryStoreType or /dev/null URL
Use NSPersistentStoreDescription with url = URL(fileURLWithPath: "/dev/null")
Explains /dev/null vs NSInMemoryStoreType difference (cascading deletes break with in-memory store)
Shows shared NSManagedObjectModel optimization (static lazy property to avoid reloading .momd per test)
Warns about cross-target namespace issue (auto-detection of model by name fails in test targets)

evals.name: integration-testing-complex
evals.prompt: Our app stores auth tokens in Keychain. Tests randomly fail because Keychain state persists between test runs. We also have UserDefaults and SwiftData. How do we fully isolate each test?

assertions.text: Keychain state persists across app installs on simulator - may need manual clear

evals.name: anti-patterns-medium

assertions.text: Identifies as a distinct anti-pattern category (assertion-free test)
Provides bash command or technique for detecting assertion-free tests in codebase
Explains the Self-validating principle (S in F.I.R.S.T.)

evals.name: anti-patterns-complex

assertions.text: #expect in XCTestCase - silently ignored (identifies as distinct anti-pattern)
Identifies pre-evaluated Bool in #expect as separate issue (distinct from framework mixing)

evals.name: test-organization-medium
evals.prompt: Our test suite takes 15 minutes. How do we speed it up?

assertions.text: Run tests in parallel (Test Plans or xcodebuild -parallel-testing-enabled)
Mentions build-for-testing/test-without-building split for CI optimization

evals.name: test-organization-complex
evals.prompt: We have flaky tests that randomly fail on CI but pass locally. They're blocking PRs. How do we handle them without just deleting them?

assertions.text: Quarantine flaky tests in separate Test Plan
Run quarantined tests but don't block PR on failure
Consider @Suite(.serialized) for Swift Testing parallel issues
